title: Cloning the Class Github Repository
author: Bonnie L Hurwitz
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.wwiffce

steps.title: Login to your Github account and...
steps.instruction: Login to your Github account and choose the "Settings" under your menu. a

steps.title: Select the SSH Keys section on...
steps.instruction: Select the SSH Keys section on the left (1) and then click on "New SSH key" in the upper-right (2). a

steps.title: Paste the public key (~/
steps.instruction: Paste the public key (~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub) from above into the "Key" on the Github site and give the key a title like "UA HPC," then click on the big green "Add SSH Key" button. a

steps.title: Clone your project via SSH
steps.instruction: Clone your project via SSH. On the UA HPC web portal, go to the "/rsgrps/bh_class/username" directory and clone your version of the biosys-analytics repository. Replace "username" with YOUR username in each of the Unix commands below. $ cd /rsgrps/bh_class/username $ git clone git@github.com:username/biosys-analytics.git

steps.title: You should now be able to...
steps.instruction: You should now be able to push/pull from your repo without having to type your Github password.

steps.title: Login to the UA HPC web portal
steps.instruction: Login to the UA HPC web portal. See the protocol if needed.

steps.title: Go to the
steps.instruction: Go to the .ssh directory in your home directory and copy the SSH key, using the commands below. $ cd $ cd .ssh $ cat id_rsa.pub copy the public key that you get from the command above.
